weakness_analysis: v5 improved from v4's 0.762 to 0.781. The code block requirement works — all 3 Priority sections in both articles have fenced code blocks. actix-actix-web.md is strong on examples (0.80): real file:line citations, verbatim code from scan, clean BEFORE/AFTER pairs. trpc-trpc.md drags examples down (0.65): two problems. (1) ANSI terminal escape codes ([?25l[?25h) contaminate the entire file — likely from ollama streaming to a non-TTY context. (2) BEFORE blocks use reconstructed/speculative code rather than verbatim scan data: '// the constructor might accept an arbitrary string' and '// In packages/.../TRPCError.ts, the constructor might...' — this is not real scan data, it's inference. The existing 'Do not invent' rule doesn't block this because the model frames it as a description of likely code, not outright fabrication. trpc also uses 'Overall Conclusion' instead of the required '## 📊 Severity Assessment' section header (template deviation).
hypothesis: Add a verbatim-only rule to Code Citation Rules: 'BEFORE code must be text you can copy-paste verbatim from the scan JSON. If you cannot paste it verbatim, use the callout. Explicit prohibition: do not write BEFORE blocks containing speculative language like "might", "probably", "example:", "the code might look like", or any phrasing that indicates the code was inferred rather than extracted.' Also reinforce the Severity Assessment section header requirement in Template Enforcement.
